info.title: SleepHQ
info.description: SleepHQ is an online platform for CPAP users to view, share and analyse their data to get the best night's sleep possible. Visit https://sleephq.com for more details.

tags.name: Current User
tags.description: Endpoints relating to the current user as defined by the API key being used

tags.name: Teams
tags.description: Teams are the internal representation of accounts in the system. Each user can belong to multiple teams and each team can have multiple users

tags.name: Patients
tags.description: For SleepHQ Consult accounts, query information about your patients with this endpoint

tags.name: Machines
tags.description: The Machine class represents both CPAP and pulse oximeters (eg O2 Rings) in the system.

tags.name: Machine Dates
tags.description: The MachineDate class represents a particular date of data for a given machine.

tags.name: Imports
tags.description: The Import class represents a single instance of a data import for a device. Each import will have one or more files associated with it

tags.name: Import Files
tags.description: Import Files represent each individual file that is uploaded for a given import.

tags.name: Journals
tags.description: There is one journal entry per user per day and it can be used to track metrics outside of devices like weight, feeling score, etc.

tags.name: Devices
tags.description: The Device class represents the type of device being used. For example, ResMed AirSense 11.

parameters.description: The device type that the data is from. Find a list of device ids using the /devices endpoint.

parameters.name: mask_id
parameters.description: The mask used for this import. Find the team's masks using the /teams/:team_id/masks endpoint.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the import to show in the UI.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

get.description: List sleep tests for a team (clinic or enterprise — enterprise rolls up across child clinics)

parameters.name: bucket
parameters.description: Filter to one of the clinic dashboard pipeline columns. `unstarted` (no recordings yet, status New), `in_progress` (recording in flight), `scorable` (all nights captured, awaiting report), `scored` (report locked / signed).

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

parameters.name: purge_files
parameters.description: If true, the files associated with the import will be deleted. Defaults to false.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The path to the file relative to the SD card root.
parameters.example: "./" for a file that is at the root. "./DATALOG/20230924/" for a file in a DATALOG folder. Note do not include the quote marks (")

parameters.name: file
parameters.description: The file object. Optional if the file has already been uploaded.

parameters.name: content_hash
parameters.description: The calculated content_hash of the file. See the /v1/imports/files/calculate_content_hash endpoint for more information

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

post.description: Start processing the files from the import

get.description: Retrieve a File. Note: the download_url param is a signed URL that will expire in 5 minutes.

post.description:
Calculate the content hash of a file when testing your content_hash algorithm.
Note - do not use this end point to calculate the content in your finished application.
This end point is only for use when validating your own implementation of the content_hash algorithm.
Excessive use of this end point may result in your account being rate limited.
The content_hash is a MD5 hash of the file's content and name joined together.
For example, if the file's name is "file.txt" and the file's content is "Hello, World!",
the content_hash would be the MD5 hash of the string "Hello, World!file.txt"
Do not include the file's path when calculating the content hash.

parameters.name: file
parameters.description: The file to calculate the content hash for

parameters.name: sort_order
parameters.description: Sort Order defaults to desc so the most recent record will be first

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

post.description: Batch create files for an Uploader or Import

parameters.name: added_by_id
parameters.description: The serial number of the uploader adding the file or the ID of the Import

parameters.name: added_by_type
parameters.description: The type of the added_by object. Either 'Uploader' or 'Import'

201.description: Batch create files for an Uploader or Import

parameters.name: blob[filename]
parameters.description: The name of the file including extension.

parameters.name: blob[content_type]
parameters.description: The content type of the file.

parameters.name: blob[byte_size]
parameters.description: The size of the file in bytes.

parameters.name: blob[checksum]
parameters.description: The base64 encoded MD5 checksum of the file.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address of the user to look up

attributes.sleep_stages: Soluta et neque. Tempora totam doloribus. Occaecati quibusdam quo.
